task: navigate: Search for round-trip flights from San Francisco to Singapore, departing on April 15, 2025, and returning on April 22, 2025, while ensuring the flights do not exceed two layovers.
expected_behavior: The agent should continue until the Google Flights results satisfy the requested route, dates, and no-more-than-two-layovers constraint.
actual_behavior: The trajectory ends with an exit message before the fixture contains evidence that the layover constraint was checked.

evidence.claim: The source task explicitly requires round-trip flights from San Francisco to Singapore with no more than two layovers.
Step 11 is an exit message rather than an action that verifies or filters layovers.
Failure memory case fm_early_terminated_001 describes this premature-exit pattern.

regression_rule: For flight-search tasks with explicit constraints, the agent must not exit until the final visible state or tool evidence confirms every route, date, and layover constraint.
